hopf_bundle_analysis.notes: Chern-Weil: curvature F is EML-2 (from connection 1-form). c_k = tr(F^k): EML-2 (max for rank=1). Integration ∫ ch → integer: EML-0 (topological invariant). The Chern-Weil map is the EML depth bridge from differential to algebraic topology.

instanton_bundle_analysis.notes: Chern-Weil: curvature F is EML-2 (from connection 1-form). c_k = tr(F^k): EML-4 (max for rank=2). Integration ∫ ch → integer: EML-0 (topological invariant). The Chern-Weil map is the EML depth bridge from differential to algebraic topology.

hopf_bundle.transition_functions: EML depth = depth of group coordinate functions
hopf_bundle.notes: Connection: EML-1 (smooth 1-form valued in Lie algebra). Curvature F=dA+[A,A]: EML-2. Chern-Simons Ω=A∧dA+(2/3)A∧A∧A: EML-3 (triple product). Topological invariants (∫ch, ∫p): EML-0 (integers). The gauge field depth ladder: A (EML-1) → F (EML-2) → CS (EML-3) → number (EML-0).

key_theorem.name: EML Depth in Algebraic Topology
key_theorem.statement: All purely combinatorial/algebraic invariants of a topological space (Betti numbers, Euler characteristic, homology groups, Chern numbers, Pontryagin numbers) are EML-0: they are integers. Differential-geometric constructions live at EML-2 (curvature) or EML-3 (Chern character). The Chern-Weil map ∫_M ch(E) integrates EML-2k forms to EML-0 integers.
key_theorem.insight: Algebraic topology collapses EML depth to 0 by integration. The journey is: EML-1 (gauge field) → EML-2 (curvature) → EML-3 (Chern char) → EML-0 (topological number, via integration over closed manifold). This mirrors the stat mech ladder: EML-1 (Boltzmann) → EML-2 (free energy) → EML-0 (phase space volume / partition structure).

summary.title: Algebraic Topology — EML Complexity of CW Complexes and Characteristic Classes

findings.name: All algebraic topology invariants are EML-0
findings.content: Betti numbers, Euler characteristic, homology groups, Chern numbers, Pontryagin numbers: all integers, EML-0.

findings.name: Gauge field depth ladder: A(1) → F(2) → ch(3) → ∫ch(0)
findings.content: Connection 1-form is EML-1. Curvature F=dA+A∧A is EML-2. Chern character ch=tr(exp(iF)) is EML-3. Integration to Chern number: EML-0.

findings.name: Chern-Weil map: EML-2k → EML-0
findings.content: The Chern-Weil map sends curvature k-forms (EML-2k) to integer topological invariants (EML-0). Integration is the EML depth collapse.

findings.name: Morse theory: topology EML-0, geometry EML(f)
findings.content: Morse indices and flow line counts are EML-0 (integers). The Morse function formula determines gradient flow EML depth. Topology is independent of differential-geometric choice.

findings.name: Non-smooth spaces: EML-inf at singularities
findings.content: A manifold with cone singularity has EML-inf at the cone point (non-analytic distance function). Smooth manifolds: EML-finite everywhere.
